answer: The September 15, 2026 Florida escrow reconciliation overstatement was caused by the settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 pipeline joining party_identity_bridge using normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id. Because normalized_tax_id can appear on multiple buyer, seller, trust, and entity roles within a single closing, the join duplicated escrow disbursements in finance.disbursement_recon_daily. The affected KPI was escrow disbursement reconciliation variance for Florida purchase closings under FNF National Title. Remediation involved switching to closing_party_role_id, quarantining normalized_tax_id joins, and backfilling the impacted data.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joined party_identity_bridge by normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id, causing duplicate escrow disbursements because the same tax ID can appear on multiple party roles within a single closing
